Home / Services / Backsplash Tile

Backsplash Tile

Statement walls designed for Atlanta's cooking styles.

A backsplash in Atlanta needs to be equal parts style and substance. We design splash zones that protect walls from frying oil splatter while elevating your kitchen's aesthetic. For Buckhead's contemporary homes, we might install large-format porcelain with minimal grout lines. In Virginia-Highland's bungalows, handmade subway tile replicates historic charm with modern ease.

Our installers are artists with trowels - creating perfect patterns despite Atlanta's often uneven walls. We use epoxy grout in high-splash zones (near stoves and prep sinks) to prevent mildew in humid months. For homes near Krog Street Market, we frequently incorporate local artisan tiles depicting Georgia motifs - peaches, dogwoods, or abstract BeltLine interpretations.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Wall surface preparation (repairing Atlanta's common plaster irregularities)
  2. 02 Step 2: Custom layout planning highlighting your kitchen's best angles
  3. 03 Step 3: Precision installation with moisture-resistant adhesives
  4. 04 Step 4: Grout selection and sealing for your cooking habits

Benefits

  • Easy-wipe surfaces for Georgia's sticky summer cooking
  • Historic reproduction tiles for preservation projects
  • Creative solutions for awkward Atlanta kitchen walls
  • Integrated outlets and switch plates for seamless looks

Frequently Asked Questions

What tile stands up to Atlanta's humidity?

Glass and porcelain tiles resist moisture best. For historic homes, we use cement tiles sealed with penetrating protectants that don't alter their matte finish.

Can you tile around my uneven old Atlanta walls?

Absolutely. Our craftsmen expertly vary mortar thickness to create flawless surfaces. In extreme cases, we might float walls first - a common need in Candler Park's vintage cottages.

Get a Free Estimate
